icons.bold_switch: When text becomes bold, icon weight also automatically thickens.
icons.doc_ref: For specific usage of the `Icon` component and a list of available icons, please see the Icon Component Documentation.
icons.feedback_clear: Provides clear visual feedback for the selected state.
icons.ghost_btn_reason: Matches border texture for a clean look.
icons.guide_title_desc: Guide for icon design principles, naming conventions, and usage guidelines across the app.
icons.heavy_weight_reason: Suits important actions needing strong attention.
icons.line_match: Matches icon stroke weight with other UI elements and borders.
icons.line_or_fill: There is no single correct answer to 'Line or Fill?'. It's important to differentiate them based on the app's nature and context where the icon is placed.
icons.mainstream_style_desc: Mainstream style: Outline for base, Filled for selected/important actions.
icons.no_mixing_desc: Avoid confusion; unify parallel features with same style.
icons.primary_btn_reason: Visibility needs to match button strength.
icons.radius_sync_desc: Match icon roundness to component roundness (Round vs Sharp).
icons.size_match: Icons automatically adjust to match the text font size.
icons.vis_priority_reason: Recognizable even when small due to silhouette.
icons.optical_alignment_desc: Adjust icons where the geometric center feels off (e.g., play button) to ensure they look visually centered.
icons.simplicity_desc: Strip unnecessary details to ensure icons remain recognizable at small sizes (16-24px).
icons.naming_conv_desc: Unify naming as `[Name]Icon` (e.g., SettingsIcon) to improve predictability within the codebase.
icons.accessibility_desc: Consider using `aria-label` or tooltips since icons alone may sometimes be ambiguous.
